Command Center› P-Rating System
P-Rating System
Productivity scoring foundation · P0–P8 scale · All thresholds configurable
Future mode — planned migration path
Baseline Standard
P8
100% of norms · confirmed
Auto-Score Ceiling
P6
~90% · calculated by system
Approval Required
P7 & P8
Workflow + manager sign-off
Zero Output
P0
Safety incident · absence · no output
Productivity Scale
P0 = no output → P8 = 100% standard norms
⚑ Requires workflow approval (P7, P8) Thresholds marked as estimates
Rating % of Norms System Behaviour Meaning Notes
P7 & P8 — Approval Workflow
Not calculated by scoring engine · requires multi-level manager sign-off
P7
P7 — Above Norms (~95%)
Requires approval from Site/Cluster Manager, Construction Head, and Division Head. Not generated by automatic PI scoring.
P8
P8 — Standard Norms (100%)
Same workflow as P7, plus Finance Manager approval. Awarded based on revenue, profitability, or exceptional performance evidence.
Approval Chain
Step 1
Site / Cluster Manager
›
Step 2
Construction Head
›
Step 3
Division Head
›
Step 4 · P8 only
Finance Manager
Example Calculation
Flow Line Route Grading · Activity Master norm = 0.375 Km/hr
Activity norm (P8 = 100%)
0.375 Km/hr
From Activity Master col M
Crew actual output
0.375 Km/hr
P8
100% · Standard norms
Crew actual output
0.300 Km/hr
P5
80% · Near norms
Crew actual output
0.056 Km/hr
P0
0% · No output / auto-triggered
0–100 Continuous Scale
Planned future migration · system designed to support both modes
Future Direction
0102030405060708090100
Migration Notes
• The 0–100 continuous score maps directly to P-levels at current thresholds (e.g., score 90 = P6, score 100 = P8)
• Removes step-function jumps — a crew scoring 72% gets 72, not P4 (60%) or P5 (80%)
• Incentive calculations remain threshold-based OR can use the raw score directly
• The configurable admin panel below supports both modes simultaneously
• No system rebuild required — the P-level thresholds become "bracket labels" on the same underlying 0–100 score
⚙ Admin: Configure P-Level Thresholds
All thresholds are adjustable — changes take effect immediately across all calculations
Configurable
Demo talking point: "Your team can adjust every threshold from here. As you fine-tune the norms, the system adapts immediately — no code changes required." Only P8 = 100% is locked (confirmed by Anand Sir, 8 Apr). All other values are estimates and fully editable.

Weak correlation (fraud signal)
Anomaly Crews
3
CW-002 · CW-003 · CW-005
CW-003 (Bravo Welding) — Day 8 reported 165% of norm (FWP-0702, NMR-D-0428). Physically impossible given shift hours and crew size. Incentive overpaid: ~$3,200 on that entry alone.
IMPOSSIBLE RATE
CW-002 (Delta Welding) — Days 18–20 output jumped to 2.3× their 17-day average with no change in crew or conditions. Estimated over-claim: ~$8,400.
SEQUENTIAL INFLATION
CW-007 (Echo Civil) — Days 12–16 reported exactly 94.0% of norm every single day. Zero natural variance across 5 consecutive days is statistically improbable (p < 0.001). Possible manual entry cloning.
PATTERN ANOMALY
Anomaly Detection 7

Incentive Cost by Crew
20-day total · ranked highest to lowest
Incentive Cost by Well
Total paid · cost per unit of output
Daily Incentive Spend Trend
Total crew incentive payments over 20 days · flagged days highlighted
AI Insight — Weak Incentive–Completion Correlation (r² = 0.38)
A healthy system would show r² > 0.75 — crews earning higher incentives genuinely completing more work faster. The current r² of 0.38 indicates incentive spend and actual well completion are only weakly linked. NMR-D-0428 has the highest incentive cost per unit ($1,847/joint) yet the slowest completion rate — a strong signal of inflated reporting rather than genuine productivity. NMR-A-0142 shows the expected pattern: higher incentive pay correlating with genuine P8 achievement and ahead-of-schedule progress.
Incentive Cost/Unit vs Completion Rate
Each point = one well · size = total incentive paid · colour = anomaly status
Incentive Spend vs Actual Progress
Dual axis · the gap between rising cost and flat progress is the inflation signal
Per-Crew Incentive Efficiency
Incentive paid per percentage point of well progress achieved — lower = more efficient
